title: `buildah run container bash`  fails with runc error
body:
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
**Description**
I'm trying to reproduce the [tutorial ](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/docs/tutorials/01-intro.md) part for starting a container with bash from a registry.  While doing so buildah exits with a error and shows the error output of runc for incorrect usage because of an undefined flag. I'm using the official package from the project atomic ppa on Ubuntu 16.04 LTS.

The parameters passed to runc, as I can sse them with the debug option enabled, are the following:

> DEBU[0000] Running ["runc" "create" "--bundle" "/tmp/buildah128430701" "--pid-file" "/tmp/buildah128430701/pid" "--no-new-keyring" "--console-socket" "/tmp/buildah128430701/console.sock" "buildah-buildah128430701"]


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Install buildah as well as runc via apt as described on the project atomic page
2. install an image via `container=$(buildah from fedora)`
3. try to run the container via `buildah run $container bash`
`

**Describe the results you received:*
buildah exits with exit status one. The help page of runc for incorrect usage is displayed with the following additional information: 

> flag provided but not defined: -console-socket

**Describe the results you expected:**
A running container with a shell prompt, as described in the tutorial.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/xenial,now 1.6-1~dev~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa8 amd64  [installiert]

```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.6-dev
Go Version:      go1.10.4
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
Git Commit:      
Built:           Tue Dec 18 16:47:02 2018
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64

```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DSU_ISO_KEY=b3acdff9588213007bdc852fd8064485
DSU_ISO_SRC=hwe-netboot-20101020ubuntu451.23
DSU_ISO_MILESTONE=2
DSU_PARTMAN_METHOD=crypto
DSU_INSTALL_DATE=Tue, 19 Feb 2019 12:47:43 +0000
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=16.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=xenial
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 16.04.5 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="16.04.5 LTS (Xenial Xerus)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 16.04.5 LTS"
VERSION_ID="16.04"
HOME_URL="http://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="http://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="http://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
VERSION_CODENAME=xenial
UBUNTU_CODENAME=xenial

```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux rbl8u1ku 4.15.0-43-generic #46~16.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Fri Dec 7 13:31:08 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary read-write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional read-only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers (currently overlay, zfs, vfs, btrfs)
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"

```

comments.body: Works now. I removed the system installation of runC (which was rc2) and installed the latest release (rc6) manually. Now the flag is known. It may be a good idea for the ppa provided package to check for the version of runC and not just the existence of runC itself, but I guess this is not the right place for this.
@SveWoCo thanks for the issue and the follow up.  @lsm5 is there anything that can be adjusted in the ppa to help with this?
